message_text:
Your buyers budgeted for the mortgage. Did anyone mention the other $21,400/year?

That's Bankrate's 2025 figure for average annual homeownership costs beyond the mortgage — utilities, maintenance, insurance, and more. In high-cost states it hits $29,000. And 42% of homeowners say hidden costs were their biggest regret about buying.

HVAC age, water heater condition, insulation levels, appliance efficiency — these drive monthly costs, but they're rarely part of the listing conversation. Pearl SCORE™'s Operations pillar surfaces them early, so agents can help buyers compare homes on total cost of ownership, not just list price.

pearlscore.com/news/what-every-buyer-needs-to-know-about-operating-costs

Two homes at $400,000. Same square footage. Same neighborhood. But one costs thousands more per year to run because of an aging HVAC system, minimal insulation, and an inefficient water heater. Your buyers won't see that difference in a listing.

Bankrate puts average annual costs beyond the mortgage at $21,400 — but the gap between an efficient home and an inefficient one can be dramatic. Pearl SCORE™'s Operations pillar makes that gap visible early, so agents can help buyers compare total cost of ownership, not just sticker price.
